keywords.name: I'm general Baa'leal. What do you want in Mal'ouquah?
keywords.general: I'm general Baa'leal. What do you want in Mal'ouquah?
keywords.baa'leal: That is GENERAL Baa'leal for you, human.
keywords.job: I am commander-in-chief of the armed forces of the UDLA, all branches of service. ...
keywords.udla: Yes. The United Djinn Liberation Army. ...
keywords.ubaid: Ubaid told you to speak with me? Hmmm... maybe there is something you could help us with. Are you interested, human?
keywords.work: Did you find the thief of our supplies?
keywords.operation: Did you already talk to Alesar? He has another mission for you!
keywords.mission: Did you already talk to Alesar? He has another mission for you!
keywords.thief: Did you find the thief of our supplies?
keywords.mal'ouquah: At the moment Mal'ouquah is our headquarter. However, I am already working on a cunning plan to move our base of operations deep into the enemy's territory.
keywords.ashta'daramai: Ashta'daramai is the enemy's base of operations. I am looking forward to the moment when we raise our flag there!
keywords.gabel: He is weak. Much too weak to be our leader.
keywords.king: The UDLA does not serve a king because there isn't any. Of course, that is bound to change.
keywords.djinn: We are a race of warriors! We Efreets are destined to rule and to conquer.
keywords.efreet: We are the true djinn! We do not live in denial of our true nature like those damn liberals, the Marid.
keywords.marid: Nothing but a bunch of mealy-mouthed, mollycoddled wimps and milksops the lot of them. They may be superior in numbers, but we will win anyway because of our superior strategic thinking.
keywords.human: No offence, but your race is weak. You lack both the physical strength and the true warrior spirit. And worst of all, you have no strategic thinking.
keywords.zathroth: I understand he created us. Must have been a great general.
keywords.tibia: It is our mission to achieve total and decisive dominion of this world within two years. Well perhaps ... three. Always be realistic, that's what I say.
keywords.daraman: Damn that liberal peacenik, that treacherous mealy-mouthed double-faced good-for-nothing surrender monkey! ...
keywords.darashia: The humans living in the northern deserts used to be nomads. Even though they are just humans they used to be respectable fighters. ...
keywords.scarab: Impressive animals. I have this idea of training them as battle steeds. Imagine this: Djinns mounted on scarabs! With a battalion of those I would crush the enemy in the blink of an eye!
keywords.edron: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.thais: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.venore: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.kazordoon: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.carlin: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.ab'dendriel: They say the humans have built some big cities over there. I am looking forward to see them burn.
keywords.ankrahmun: That old city has some impressive defensive structures. But I swear I will bring it down one day... I have a cunning plan already! ...
keywords.pharaoh: Ankrahmun's pharaoh apparently believes himself to be some sort of god. Ah well. A solid blow with my scimitar will bring him back to earth soon enough!
keywords.palace: I suppose the palace is where the pharaoh resides. I have a distinct feeling I shall see it burn rather soon.
keywords.ascension: Apparently, ascension is what the followers of the pharaoh are after. No idea what exactly that is, though.
keywords.kha'zeel: It was an excellent idea to build our headquarter in the mountains of kha'zeel. Easy to defend, you know. Too bad the enemy had the same idea.
keywords.kha'labal: Kha'labal? Yes, it was me who devastated it. Couldn't leave it to the enemy, you see? We had to destroy it in order to save it!
keywords.war: War is the father of things, and I live and breathe it. Ok, it's a tad bit silly that we are forced to fight against our own kind, but as a good soldier I will do my duty! <salutes> ...
keywords.melchior: Melchior! I remember that greedy little civilian. I would have court-martialled him, but I suppose it is just as well the way it is.
keywords.alesar: Ah yes, Alesar! Excellent smith, that man!
keywords.fa'hradin: He is Gabel's lieutenant and confidant. He is a powerful wizard, one has to admit that - and that's the only reason he is still alive. Without all his magical mumbo jumbo we would have long since won this war.
keywords.lamp: We sleep in those lamps. I like them - they are small and functional. We do not need cozy beds and fluffy duvets like decadent humans.

texts: You know the code human! Very well then... What do you want, ?
You are still alive, ? Well, what do you want?
I am commander-in-chief of the armed forces of the UDLA, all branches of service. ...

greetingPatterns.response: You know the code human! Very well then... What do you want, %N?
You are still alive, %N? Well, what do you want?

entries.response: Well ... All right. You may only be a human, but you do seem to have the right spirit. ...
After all, you're just a human.
Finally! What is his name then?
Then go to Carlin and search for him! Look for something that might give you a clue!
You found the thief! Excellent work, soldier! You are doing well - for a human, that is. Here - take this as a reward. ...
Hmmm... I don't think so. Return to Carlin and continue your search!
